headline: Microsoft Africa AI Push And Cloud Spend Weighed Against Valuation Upside
summary: Microsoft is expanding its AI footprint in Africa, with plans to train 3,000,000 people in AI skills. The company is partnering with MTN Group to bring Microsoft 365 and Copilot AI tools to 300,000,000 subscribers. Microsoft has announced a US$330 million investment to grow cloud and data center capacity in Africa, including exploring renewable powered infrastructure in Kenya. For investors tracking Microsoft (NasdaqGS:MSFT), the AI push in Africa comes as the stock trades at a recent close...

headline: NVIDIA Joins OCI To Shape Optical Links And AI Data Center Future
summary: NVIDIA (NasdaqGS:NVDA) has co founded the Optical Compute Interconnect (OCI) Multi Source Agreement to define open standards for optical links in AI data centers. The consortium includes AMD, Broadcom, Meta, Microsoft, and OpenAI, aiming to create interoperable, high density optical interconnects. The group intends to address copper connectivity bottlenecks and support much larger AI clusters for future workloads. NVIDIA enters this effort with a strong market profile and a current share...

headline: EXCLUSIVE: Just 5 Female CEOs In A 50-Stock ETF — C-Suite Execs Explain The Leadership Gap In Corporate America
summary: -link data-ticker=GOOGL data-exchange=NASDAQ href=https://www.benzinga.com/quote/GOOGL target=_blank rel=noopener>(NASDAQ: GOOGL), One week after we celebrated the International Women's day, here is a stark reminder how the C-suite remains underrepresented by women.Data from a leadership-focused ETF underscores how uncommon female leadership remains among large U.S. companies. The Wedbush ReturnOnLeadership U.S. Large-Cap ETF (ARCA: EXEQ) universe of 50 companies, only about 10% are led by women CEOs. Those include Jayshree Ullal of Arista Networks Inc (NYSE: ANET), Lori Koch of DuPont de Nemours Inc (NYSE: DD), Martina Cheung of S&P Global Inc (NYSE: SPGI), Ashley McEvoy of Insulet Corp (NASDAQ: PODD) and Debra Cafaro of Ventas Inc (NYSE: VTR). The ETF tracks companies ranked through a leadership execution framework developed by Indiggo. Janeen Gelbart, co-founder and CEO of the firm, said the methodology evaluates corporate leadership broadly rather than focusing on individual executives.

raw.headline: EXCLUSIVE: Just 5 Female CEOs In A 50-Stock ETF — C-Suite Execs Explain The Leadership Gap In Corporate America
raw.summary: -link data-ticker=GOOGL data-exchange=NASDAQ href=https://www.benzinga.com/quote/GOOGL target=_blank rel=noopener>(NASDAQ: GOOGL), One week after we celebrated the International Women's day, here is a stark reminder how the C-suite remains underrepresented by women.Data from a leadership-focused ETF underscores how uncommon female leadership remains among large U.S. companies. The Wedbush ReturnOnLeadership U.S. Large-Cap ETF (ARCA: EXEQ) universe of 50 companies, only about 10% are led by women CEOs. Those include Jayshree Ullal of Arista Networks Inc (NYSE: ANET), Lori Koch of DuPont de Nemours Inc (NYSE: DD), Martina Cheung of S&P Global Inc (NYSE: SPGI), Ashley McEvoy of Insulet Corp (NASDAQ: PODD) and Debra Cafaro of Ventas Inc (NYSE: VTR). The ETF tracks companies ranked through a leadership execution framework developed by Indiggo. Janeen Gelbart, co-founder and CEO of the firm, said the methodology evaluates corporate leadership broadly rather than focusing on individual executives.

headline: Goldman: Software giants face ‘radical transformation’ as agentic AI rises
summary: Investing.com -- The software sector, once the primary engine of global digital transformation, is facing a significant challenge as "agentic" artificial intelligence tools threaten to disrupt established business models. According to a new Goldman Sachs "Top of Mind" report, the rise of AI capable of autonomous software development has fueled investor fears that the technology may "eat" the very industry that pioneered it, leading to a sharp re-rating of software stocks.

headline: Broadcom’s OCI Push Puts AI Data Center Role In Focus
summary: Broadcom (NasdaqGS:AVGO) has introduced a 400G/lane optical DSP and is leading the new Optical Compute Interconnect, or OCI, open standard alongside AMD, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, and OpenAI. The company is also working with JetCool and Flex to bring production-ready liquid cooling solutions into AI data center deployments. These developments aim to support hyperscalable AI clusters with multivendor optical connectivity and advanced thermal management. Broadcom is widely known for its...
